verses.kjv: PAUL, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God’s elect, and the acknowledging of the truth which is after godliness;
verses.bsb: Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ for the faith of God’s elect and their knowledge of the truth that leads to godliness,

verses.kjv: In hope of eternal life, which God, that cannot lie, promised before the world began;
verses.bsb: in the hope of eternal life, which God, who cannot lie, promised before time began.

verses.kjv: But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching, which is committed unto me according to the commandment of God our Saviour;
verses.bsb: In His own time He has made His word evident in the proclamation entrusted to me by the command of God our Savior.

verses.kjv: To Titus, mine own son after the common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Saviour.
verses.bsb: To Titus, my true child in our common faith:  Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior.

verses.kjv: For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest set in order the things that are wanting, and ordain elders in every city, as I had appointed thee:
verses.bsb: The reason I left you in Crete was that you would set in order what was unfinished and appoint elders in every town, as I directed you.

verses.kjv: If any be blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children not accused of riot or unruly.
verses.bsb: An elder must be blameless, the husband of but one wife, having children who are believers and who are not open to accusation of indiscretion or insubordination.

verses.kjv: For a bishop must be blameless, as the steward of God; not selfwilled, not soon angry, not given to wine, no striker, not given to filthy lucre;
verses.bsb: As God’s steward, an overseer must be above reproach—not self-absorbed, not quick-tempered, not given to drunkenness, not violent, not greedy for money.

verses.kjv: But a lover of hospitality, a lover of good men, sober, just, holy, temperate;
verses.bsb: Instead, he must be hospitable, a lover of good, self-controlled, upright, holy, and disciplined.

verses.kjv: Holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught, that he may be able by sound doctrine both to exhort and to convince the gainsayers.
verses.bsb: He must hold firmly to the faithful word as it was taught, so that he can encourage others by sound teaching and refute those who contradict it.

verses.kjv: For there are many unruly and vain talkers and deceivers, specially they of the circumcision:
verses.bsb: For many are rebellious and full of empty talk and deception, especially those of the circumcision,

verses.kjv: Whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for filthy lucre’s sake.
verses.bsb: who must be silenced. For the sake of dishonorable gain, they undermine entire households and teach things they should not.

verses.kjv: One of themselves, even a prophet of their own, said, The Cretians are alway liars, evil beasts, slow bellies.
verses.bsb: As one of their own prophets has said, “Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, lazy gluttons.”

verses.kjv: This witness is true. Wherefore rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith;
verses.bsb: This testimony is true. Therefore rebuke them sternly, so that they will be sound in the faith

verses.kjv: Not giving heed to Jewish fables, and commandments of men, that turn from the truth.
verses.bsb: and will pay no attention to Jewish myths or to the commands of men who have rejected the truth.

verses.kjv: Unto the pure all things are pure: but unto them that are defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure; but even their mind and conscience is defiled.
verses.bsb: To the pure, all things are pure; but to the defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure. Indeed, both their minds and their consciences are defiled.

verses.kjv: They profess that they know God; but in works they deny him, being abominable, and disobedient, and unto every good work reprobate.
verses.bsb: They profess to know God, but by their actions they deny Him. They are detestable, disobedient, and unfit for any good deed.

verses.kjv: BUT speak thou the things which become sound doctrine:
verses.bsb: But as for you, speak the things that are consistent with sound doctrine.

verses.kjv: That the aged men be sober, grave, temperate, sound in faith, in charity, in patience.
verses.bsb: Older men are to be temperate, dignified, self-controlled, and sound in faith, love, and perseverance.

verses.kjv: The aged women likewise, that they be in behaviour as becometh holiness, not false accusers, not given to much wine, teachers of good things;
verses.bsb: Older women, likewise, are to be reverent in their behavior, not slanderers or addicted to much wine, but teachers of good.

verses.kjv: That they may teach the young women to be sober, to love their husbands, to love their children,
verses.bsb: In this way they can train the young women to love their husbands and children,

verses.kjv: To be discreet, chaste, keepers at home, good, obedient to their own husbands, that the word of God be not blasphemed.
verses.bsb: to be self-controlled, pure, managers of their households, kind, and submissive to their own husbands, so that the word of God will not be discredited.

verses.kjv: Young men likewise exhort to be sober minded.
verses.bsb: In the same way, urge the younger men to be self-controlled.

verses.kjv: In all things shewing thyself a pattern of good works: in doctrine shewing uncorruptness, gravity, sincerity,
verses.bsb: In everything, show yourself to be an example by doing good works. In your teaching show integrity, dignity,

verses.kjv: Sound speech, that cannot be condemned; that he that is of the contrary part may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of you.
verses.bsb: and wholesome speech that is above reproach, so that anyone who opposes us will be ashamed, having nothing bad to say about us.

verses.kjv: Exhort servants to be obedient unto their own masters, and to please them well in all things; not answering again;
verses.bsb: Slaves are to submit to their own masters in everything, to be well-pleasing, not argumentative,

verses.kjv: Not purloining, but shewing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things.
verses.bsb: not stealing from them, but showing all good faith, so that in every respect they will adorn the teaching about God our Savior.

verses.kjv: For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,
verses.bsb: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to everyone.

verses.kjv: Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;
verses.bsb: It instructs us to renounce ungodliness and worldly passions, and to live sensible, upright, and godly lives in the present age,

verses.kjv: Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ;
verses.bsb: as we await the blessed hope and glorious appearance of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ.

verses.kjv: Who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify unto himself a peculiar people, zealous of good works.
verses.bsb: He gave Himself for us to redeem us from all lawlessness and to purify for Himself a people for His own possession, zealous for good deeds.

verses.kjv: These things speak, and exhort, and rebuke with all authority. Let no man despise thee.
verses.bsb: Speak these things as you encourage and rebuke with all authority. Let no one despise you.

verses.kjv: PUT them in mind to be subject to principalities and powers, to obey magistrates, to be ready to every good work,
verses.bsb: Remind the believers to submit to rulers and authorities, to be obedient and ready for every good work,

verses.kjv: To speak evil of no man, to be no brawlers, but gentle, shewing all meekness unto all men.
verses.bsb: to malign no one, and to be peaceable and gentle, showing full consideration to everyone.

verses.kjv: For we ourselves also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving divers lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.
verses.bsb: For at one time we too were foolish, disobedient, misled, and enslaved to all sorts of desires and pleasures—living in malice and envy, being hated and hating one another.

verses.kjv: But after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared,
verses.bsb: But when the kindness of God our Savior and His love for mankind appeared,

verses.kjv: Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost;
verses.bsb: He saved us, not by the righteous deeds we had done, but according to His mercy, through the washing of new birth and renewal by the Holy Spirit.

verses.kjv: Which he shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour;
verses.bsb: This is the Spirit He poured out on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Savior,

verses.kjv: That being justified by his grace, we should be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
verses.bsb: so that, having been justified by His grace, we would become heirs with the hope of eternal life.

verses.kjv: This is a faithful saying, and these things I will that thou affirm constantly, that they which have believed in God might be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable unto men.
verses.bsb: This saying is trustworthy. And I want you to emphasize these things, so that those who have believed God will take care to devote themselves to good deeds. These things are excellent and profitable for the people.

verses.kjv: But avoid foolish questions, and genealogies, and contentions, and strivings about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.
verses.bsb: But avoid foolish controversies, genealogies, arguments, and quarrels about the law, because these things are pointless and worthless.

verses.kjv: A man that is an heretick after the first and second admonition reject;
verses.bsb: Reject a divisive man after a first and second admonition,

verses.kjv: Knowing that he that is such is subverted, and sinneth, being condemned of himself.
verses.bsb: knowing that such a man is corrupt and sinful; he is self-condemned.

verses.kjv: When I shall send Artemas unto thee, or Tychicus, be diligent to come unto me to Nicopolis: for I have determined there to winter.
verses.bsb: As soon as I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, make every effort to come to me at Nicopolis, because I have decided to winter there.

verses.kjv: Bring Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey diligently, that nothing be wanting unto them.
verses.bsb: Do your best to equip Zenas the lawyer and Apollos, so that they will have everything they need.

verses.kjv: And let ours also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they be not unfruitful.
verses.bsb: And our people must also learn to devote themselves to good works in order to meet the pressing needs of others, so that they will not be unfruitful.

verses.kjv: All that are with me salute thee. Greet them that love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen. ¶ It was written to Titus, ordained the first bishop of the church of the Cretians, from Nicopolis of Macedonia.
verses.bsb: All who are with me send you greetings.  Greet those who love us in the faith.  Grace be with all of you.
